mitre_attack: T1499.004 - Endpoint Denial of Service: Application or System Exploitation
T1498.001 - Network Denial of Service: Direct Network Flood
cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
CWE-772 - Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime

threat.title: Slowloris HTTP Connection Exhaustion Attack
threat.description: Slowloris is a low-bandwidth denial of service attack that exploits the way web servers handle concurrent connections. By sending partial HTTP requests and keeping connections open indefinitely, an attacker can exhaust the server's connection pool with minimal resources. Unlike traditional DDoS attacks, Slowloris requires only a single machine sending slow, incomplete requests to take down production web servers.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker initiates multiple HTTP connections to target web server
2. Sends partial HTTP headers (e.g., 'GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: target.com\r\n')
3. Periodically sends additional incomplete headers (e.g., 'X-a: b\r\n') every 10-15 seconds
4. Keeps connections alive indefinitely by never sending final '\r\n\r\n' terminator
5. Server's connection pool becomes exhausted (typically 150-300 max concurrent connections)
6. Legitimate users receive 'connection refused' or severe timeout errors as server cannot accept new connections
prerequisites: Target web server uses HTTP/1.1 with persistent connections enabled
Server lacks proper request timeout configurations
No connection rate limiting or request completion timeouts
Missing Layer 7 DDoS protection (WAF, CDN, or reverse proxy)

cve_references.description: Apache HTTP Server 1.x and 2.x allows remote attackers to cause denial of service via partial HTTP requests (Slowloris attack)
Apache HTTP Server 2.4.x before 2.4.10 allows remote attackers to cause denial of service via slow HTTP requests
Apache HTTP Server 2.4 mod_http2 vulnerable to slow read DoS attack via HTTP/2 requests
HTTP/2 internal data buffering leading to denial of service (similar to Slowloris but HTTP/2)

real_world_breaches.impact: Multiple music industry websites taken offline for 48+ hours using Slowloris attacks
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Apache servers with default configurations vulnerable to connection exhaustion

real_world_breaches.incident: Iranian Election Protests - Government Website Attacks
real_world_breaches.impact: Multiple Iranian government websites disrupted using Slowloris during election protests
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Unprotected Apache servers without request timeout configurations

real_world_breaches.impact: Documented 100% success rate in blocking Slowloris attacks across 2 million+ websites
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Cloudflare implemented connection timeout enforcement and request completion validation

real_world_breaches.impact: Over 10,000 WordPress sites on shared hosting taken offline simultaneously
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Shared hosting providers using Apache MPM prefork with insufficient MaxClients configuration

security_research.title: Slowloris HTTP DoS - RSnake (Original Research)
security_research.key_finding: Demonstrated that a single machine could take down Apache servers with only 100-200 connections

security_research.title: Mitigating Slow HTTP DoS Attacks in Apache HTTP Server 2.4
security_research.key_finding: mod_reqtimeout module effectively prevents Slowloris by enforcing header and body read timeouts

security_research.title: OWASP Denial of Service Cheat Sheet - Slow HTTP Attacks
security_research.key_finding: Slowloris remains one of the most effective application-layer DoS attacks due to low resource requirements

mitigations.title: nginx Connection and Request Timeout Configuration
mitigations.description: Configure nginx with aggressive timeout policies to terminate slow or incomplete HTTP requests before connection pool exhaustion. This is the most effective defense against Slowloris attacks in production environments.

tools.name: nginx
tools.cost: Free (Open Source) / nginx Plus starts at $2,500/year

iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Verify nginx config syntax: nginx -t
Test connection timeout with: curl --limit-rate 1 http://example.com
Monitor active connections: netstat -an | grep :80 | wc -l
Simulate Slowloris attack using slowhttptest tool
Verify timeout logs in /var/log/nginx/error.log

mitigations.title: Apache mod_reqtimeout and MPM Configuration
mitigations.description: Enable Apache's mod_reqtimeout module and configure Multi-Processing Module (MPM) with appropriate connection limits to prevent Slowloris attacks.

code_example.code:
# Load mod_reqtimeout module (usually enabled by default in Apache 2.4+)
# Check with: apachectl -M | grep reqtimeout
LoadModule reqtimeout_module modules/mod_reqtimeout.so

# /etc/apache2/mods-enabled/reqtimeout.conf
<IfModule mod_reqtimeout.c>
  # Set timeout for receiving request headers
  # Minimum initial timeout 5 seconds, +1 second per 500 bytes received
  # Maximum 10 seconds total
  RequestReadTimeout header=5-10,MinRate=500
  
  # Set timeout for receiving request body
  # Minimum initial timeout 10 seconds, +1 second per 1000 bytes received
  # Maximum 30 seconds total for POST/PUT requests
  RequestReadTimeout body=10-30,MinRate=1000
</IfModule>

# /etc/apache2/apache2.conf or httpd.conf
# Configure timeouts globally
Timeout 30
KeepAliveTimeout 5
MaxKeepAliveRequests 100

# Configure MPM Event (recommended for high concurrency)
<IfModule mpm_event_module>
    StartServers             4
    MinSpareThreads          25
    MaxSpareThreads          75
    ThreadLimit              64
    ThreadsPerChild          25
    MaxRequestWorkers        400
    MaxConnectionsPerChild   1000
    
    # Limit connections per IP (requires mod_limitipconn)
    # MaxConnPerIP 10
</IfModule>

# Enable mod_evasive for additional DoS protection
LoadModule evasive20_module modules/mod_evasive20.so

<IfModule mod_evasive20.c>
    DOSHashTableSize    3097
    DOSPageCount        5
    DOSSiteCount        50
    DOSPageInterval     1
    DOSSiteInterval     1
    DOSBlockingPeriod   10
    DOSEmailNotify      security@example.com
    DOSLogDir           "/var/log/mod_evasive"
</IfModule>

# Virtual host configuration
<VirtualHost *:80>
    ServerName example.com
    DocumentRoot /var/www/html
    
    # Set connection timeout
    TimeOut 30
    
    # Limit concurrent connections from single IP
    <IfModule mod_limitipconn.c>
        <Location />
            MaxConnPerIP 10
        </Location>
    </IfModule>
    
    ErrorLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/error.log
    CustomLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/access.log combined
</VirtualHost>

nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
PR.PT-5 - Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience requirements
validation_steps: Verify mod_reqtimeout is loaded: apachectl -M | grep reqtimeout
Test Apache config: apachectl configtest
Simulate slow header attack: slowhttptest -c 200 -H -g -o slowloris.html -i 10 -r 50 -t GET -u http://example.com
Monitor server status: apachectl status
Check error logs for timeout events: tail -f /var/log/apache2/error.log

mitigations.title: HAProxy Connection Rate Limiting and Timeouts
mitigations.description: Deploy HAProxy as a reverse proxy with stick-tables for connection tracking and rate limiting to prevent Slowloris attacks before they reach backend servers.

code_example.code:
# /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg
global
    log /dev/log local0
    log /dev/log local1 notice
    chroot /var/lib/haproxy
    stats socket /run/haproxy/admin.sock mode 660 level admin
    stats timeout 30s
    user haproxy
    group haproxy
    daemon
    
    # Increase max connections
    maxconn 4096
    
    # Enable anti-DDoS features
    tune.http.max-header 100
    tune.http.max-hdr 100

defaults
    log     global
    mode    http
    option  httplog
    option  dontlognull
    
    # Aggressive timeouts for Slowloris prevention
    timeout connect 5s
    timeout client  10s    # Max time to wait for client to send request
    timeout server  30s    # Max time to wait for backend response
    timeout http-request 5s  # Max time to wait for complete HTTP request
    timeout http-keep-alive 2s
    
    # Close connections on timeout
    option http-server-close
    option forwardfor except 127.0.0.0/8
    
    errorfile 400 /etc/haproxy/errors/400.http
    errorfile 408 /etc/haproxy/errors/408.http
    errorfile 500 /etc/haproxy/errors/500.http
    errorfile 503 /etc/haproxy/errors/503.http

frontend http_front
    bind *:80
    bind *:443 ssl crt /etc/haproxy/certs/example.com.pem
    
    # Create stick-table for connection tracking
    stick-table type ip size 1m expire 30s store conn_cur,conn_rate(10s),http_req_rate(10s)
    
    # Track connections by source IP
    tcp-request connection track-sc0 src
    
    # Deny clients with too many connections (Slowloris detection)
    tcp-request connection reject if { src_conn_cur gt 10 }
    tcp-request connection reject if { src_conn_rate gt 20 }
    
    # HTTP request rate limiting
    http-request track-sc1 src
    http-request deny deny_status 429 if { sc1_http_req_rate gt 100 }
    
    # Reject requests with incomplete headers after timeout
    http-request deny deny_status 408 if { req.hdr_cnt(host) eq 0 }
    
    # Add security headers
    http-response set-header X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN
    http-response set-header X-Content-Type-Options nosniff
    
    # Route to backend
    default_backend web_servers

backend web_servers
    balance roundrobin
    option httpchk GET /health
    
    # Backend server health checks
    default-server inter 3s fall 3 rise 2
    
    # Backend servers
    server web1 10.0.1.10:8080 check maxconn 100
    server web2 10.0.1.11:8080 check maxconn 100
    server web3 10.0.1.12:8080 check maxconn 100

# Statistics page
listen stats
    bind *:8404
    stats enable
    stats uri /stats
    stats refresh 30s
    stats admin if TRUE

iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Validate HAProxy config: haproxy -c -f /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg
Test connection limiting: for i in {1..15}; do curl http://example.com & done
Monitor stick-table entries: echo 'show table http_front' | socat stdio /run/haproxy/admin.sock
Simulate Slowloris: slowhttptest -c 500 -H -g -o test.html -i 5 -r 100 -t GET -u http://example.com
Check HAProxy stats page: http://example.com:8404/stats

mitigations.title: AWS Application Load Balancer with Connection Draining
mitigations.description: Leverage AWS Application Load Balancer (ALB) with idle timeout configuration and connection draining to automatically terminate slow connections and protect backend instances.

tools.name: AWS WAF
tools.cost: $5/month per Web ACL + $1/month per rule + $0.60 per million requests

iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Apply Terraform configuration: terraform apply
Set idle timeout: aws elbv2 modify-load-balancer-attributes --load-balancer-arn <ARN> --attributes Key=idle_timeout.timeout_seconds,Value=10
Test connection timeout with slow client simulation
Monitor CloudWatch metrics: TargetConnectionErrorCount, UnHealthyHostCount
Review ALB access logs in S3 for slow connection patterns

mitigations.title: Cloudflare DDoS Protection and Rate Limiting
mitigations.description: Implement Cloudflare as a CDN and WAF to automatically detect and mitigate Slowloris attacks before they reach origin servers.
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Apply Terraform configuration: terraform apply
Enable "I'm Under Attack" mode temporarily for testing
Test rate limiting with: ab -n 1000 -c 100 https://example.com/
Review Cloudflare Analytics dashboard for threat events
Verify origin server never receives more than expected connections

mitigations.title: Node.js HTTP Server Timeout Configuration
mitigations.description: Configure Node.js HTTP server with aggressive timeout policies and connection limits to prevent Slowloris attacks on Node.js applications.
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
PR.PT-3 - Access to systems and assets is controlled
validation_steps: Install dependencies: npm install express express-rate-limit express-slow-down
Start server and monitor logs: node server.js
Test timeout with slow client: curl --limit-rate 1 http://localhost:3000
Simulate Slowloris: slowhttptest -c 200 -H -g -o test.html -i 10 -r 50 -t GET -u http://localhost:3000
Monitor active connections and verify automatic termination

indicators: Unusually high number of established connections from single IP addresses
Connections remaining in ESTABLISHED state for extended periods (>30 seconds)
HTTP requests with incomplete headers (missing final CRLF terminator)
Gradual increase in server connection pool utilization approaching max capacity
Increase in 408 Request Timeout or 503 Service Unavailable errors
Server response times degrading while CPU and memory remain low
Multiple connections from same source with minimal data transfer
Connection count spike without corresponding increase in completed requests

log_queries.query:
// Detect Slowloris attack patterns in Azure Application Gateway logs
AzureDiagnostics
| where ResourceType == "APPLICATIONGATEWAYS"
| where TimeGenerated > ago(10m)
| extend ClientIP = tostring(split(clientIP_s, ':')[0])
| summarize 
    ConnectionCount = dcount(connectionId_g),
    AvgRequestTime = avg(timeTaken_d),
    ErrorCount = countif(httpStatus_d >= 400)
    by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where ConnectionCount > 50 and AvgRequestTime > 5000
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIP, ConnectionCount, AvgRequestTime, ErrorCount
| order by ConnectionCount desc

index=web_logs sourcetype=access_combined
| stats 
    count as req_count,
    dc(session_id) as unique_sessions,
    avg(response_time) as avg_resp_time,
    max(response_time) as max_resp_time
    by src_ip, _time span=1m
| where req_count > 100 AND unique_sessions < 5 AND avg_resp_time > 5000
| eval slowloris_score = (req_count / unique_sessions) * (avg_resp_time / 1000)
| where slowloris_score > 50
| table _time, src_ip, req_count, unique_sessions, avg_resp_time, slowloris_score
| sort -slowloris_score

# Detect Slowloris patterns in ALB logs
fields @timestamp, client_ip, target_status_code, request_processing_time, target_processing_time
| filter ispresent(request_processing_time) and request_processing_time > 5
| stats count(*) as request_count, 
        avg(request_processing_time) as avg_processing_time,
        max(request_processing_time) as max_processing_time
  by client_ip, bin(5m)
| filter request_count > 50 and avg_processing_time > 5
| sort request_count desc
| limit 100

tools.name: netstat / ss
tools.usage:
netstat -an | grep :80 | grep ESTABLISHED | wc -l  # Count active connections
ss -tan state established '( dport = :80 or sport = :80 )' | wc -l

tools.name: Wireshark
tools.usage:
tcp.flags.syn==1 and tcp.flags.ack==0  # Filter SYN packets
http.request.method  # Filter HTTP requests

tools.name: Apache mod_status
tools.usage: curl http://localhost/server-status  # View active connections and requests
